risk_levels.R0: ordinary chat or stable knowledge answer
risk_levels.R2: write durable notes, handoff docs, or artifacts
risk_levels.R4: experiment, build, performance, runtime, external research, or current factual claim
risk_levels.R5: delete, commit, install, login, payment, permission, network/firewall/proxy edit, sensitive transfer, or long-term memory write

explicit_action_negation_phrases: commit and push should not run

local_project_lane_overlay.rule: Machine-local project lane and memory-root mappings may be loaded from this overlay. Do not commit private local roots into the public policy JSON.

global_memory_lane.rule: GLOBAL memory is manual-only; do not infer it from cwd unless the user explicitly requests global memory work.

router_decision_contract.purpose: keep routing and dynamic evaluation strong but low-cost before opening deeper modules
router_decision_contract.skill_lifecycle_rule: Skill lifecycle is mandatory for skill-layer work. Keep idle skills at listing/meta-summary level; load SKILL.md and needed support files only during an active frame; after a skill phase ends, release large skill body content where the host supports context GC and retain a compact skill_release_receipt with skill_id, completed_steps, current_stage, artifact_paths, evidence_refs, open_loops, and resume_entry.
router_decision_contract.feedback_loop_profile_rule: feedback_loop_profile controls only the memory -> prediction -> verification -> calibration loop depth; it is not a replacement for risk_level, external_need, claim_risk, record_intent, memory isolation, or other required_gates. Multiple gates still combine by union. Use index_hint for cheap common-error lookup hints, record_candidate for compact CE write candidates, prevention_review when selected memory should actively prevent recurrence, and explicit_cycle only when the user or task explicitly asks for the full feedback loop.
router_decision_contract.debt_hygiene_rule: When self-check shows substantial memory pollution, target pollution, dirty-tree debt, or accumulated technical debt in the current project or long conversation, run a debt hygiene pass: inventory and group issues, separate must-clean-now from deferrable debt, clean only the current required set, and mark deferred items as candidate_technical_debt for review during the next cleanup.
router_decision_contract.hybrid_retrieval_rule: Hybrid retrieval is an enhancement profile selected after memory_need. It must preserve meta-first lane/category narrowing and must not replace existing memory isolation, source-monitoring, or claim gates.
router_decision_contract.memory_write_profile_rule: When memory_mode is write or update, durable memory content must be context-complete and source-preserving. Explicit reusable memory, conversation-memory, and cross-conversation writes require strict capsule shape rather than orphan fragments.
conversation_memory_explicit_triggers: 创建此对话新记忆文件
建立此对话新记忆文件
更新当前对话本地记忆
更新当前对话的本地记忆
更新这份本地对话记忆
active_conversation_write_intent_trigger: 创建此对话新记忆文件
建立此对话新记忆文件
链接上一段对话记忆文件

conversation_memory_full_lane_triggers.decision_rule: Create or update the active conversation memory lane when an explicit conversation-memory request is present, or when any threshold group crosses its threshold in a non-read-only task. Keep conversation_memory_threshold as a backward-compatible coarse fallback.

router_decision_contract.low_cost_rule: Use microkernel -> router contract -> one matching module -> one needed gate -> one needed index -> only matching payload.

causal_attribution_contract.purpose: Output-side attribution review for high-risk causal, stability, definition, or generalization assertions. It prevents formation paths, local observations, or single cases from being written as mechanism properties or validated causality.
causal_attribution_contract.rule: Use trigger groups as a first-pass filter only. The gate should fire only on high-risk assertion patterns: abstract system/framework/mechanism subject plus causal predicate plus global effect; time-range plus stability assertion; single-sample/case wording plus generalization; or origin/path wording mixed into a mechanism definition. Scoped local causal reasoning should not trigger unless it omits the needed empirical or hypothesis boundary.
causal_attribution_contract.exclusion: This gate does not decide public/private visibility or release eligibility. Information-boundary decisions remain in the public/private boundary gate.

receipt_profiles.profile_rule: Always compute the full routing decision internally. Keep R0-R5 labels silent by default and expose only fields that change execution path, cost, permission, memory, conversation-linking, external search, or claim behavior. Expand only when governance, public/private, projectization, R4/R5, memory write, conversation-link, strong claim, or debug triggers require it.

compact_runtime.purpose: default low-cost runtime receipt for ordinary nontrivial tasks and single-user local agents

extended_governance.purpose: expanded receipt for framework rules, public repositories, adapters, project memory, conversation memory, projectization, archive, persona, and cross-boundary governance

debug_receipt.purpose: full diagnostic receipt for router development and misroute debugging

cost_control_contract.action_relevant_rule: Only emit or load a field by default if it can change the next action. Otherwise keep it in docs, archive meta, debug receipt, or audit logs.
cost_control_contract.classification_visibility_rule: R0-R5 classification always runs internally. User-facing replies and prompt-stage context stay silent unless classification changes execution path, cost, permission, memory, external search, or claim boundary. Debug or audit requests expose the full receipt.

routing_field_budget.R1_R2: usually implicit; compact_runtime only when an action-changing gate appears
routing_field_budget.R3_R4: compact_runtime plus triggered fields only; do not display risk labels unless needed for boundary explanation

cost_control_contract.delta_receipt_rule: After the initial receipt, re-evaluation should emit changed fields only unless full debug is requested.

memory_routing_contract.purpose: decide whether memory should be read, written, updated, or skipped before opening memory payloads
memory_routing_contract.default_rule: Ordinary chat and projectless small fixes do not write memory unless the user explicitly asks, an error is recurring/reusable, a conversation checkpoint is needed, or projectization drift is detected.
memory_routing_contract.explicit_record_rule: If the user says to record this error/problem/mistake or asks for self-reflection matrix recording, route to the self-reflection matrix or common error corpus according to severity.
memory_routing_contract.common_error_rule: Small but recurring execution mistakes should enter common_error_corpus as lightweight CE error-and-solution records. A CE record must include the mistake, cause, applied solution, prevention rule, validation, and evidence; only high-impact, repeated, or user-explicit incidents upgrade to paired ERR/SOL records.
memory_routing_contract.conversation_memory_rule: Projectless long-running conversations may create a current_conversation lane when the user explicitly asks for a checkpoint or when durable conversation signals cross the threshold. If an ACTIVE current_conversation lane already exists, durable decisions, reusable errors, memory schema changes, open-loop changes, or governance/routing fixes should route to memory_mode=update and memory_lane=current_conversation even when the same conversation has become project-shaped. This lane is isolated by conversation/thread id, follows meta-first retrieval, and may be read by other conversations only through explicit reference. Cross-conversation writes require explicit user instruction.
memory_routing_contract.source_monitoring_schema_rule: Reusable memory capsules should preserve source_tag, belief_status, structured confidence, derived_from, source_monitoring, lifecycle, and belief_trace_summary. Optional numeric scores are adapter metadata and must not replace confidence basis or provenance.
memory_routing_contract.retrieval_result_rule: Reusable memory retrieval results must include source_tag, derived_from, belief_status, confidence, and score_method with the selected text. If no numeric score is computed, use score_method=none and omit score.
memory_routing_contract.projectization_rule: If projectless work accumulates durable files, versioning, repository/docs, tests, adapters, releases, or repeated architecture decisions, mark emergent_project_candidate and ask or state the lane assumption before durable memory writes.

global_archive_contract.default_lookup_rule: Use active project or conversation lane first. Read the global archive only when the user asks for archive lookup, the active lane has no relevant record, or a lane is being sealed or snapshotted.
global_archive_contract.summary_capsule_rule: ARCHIVE_SUMMARY_CAPSULE requires explicit compression, migration, de-identification, public-release, or storage-reduction intent. Do not delete the source unless separately confirmed.

persona_state_policy.durable_user_profile_rule: Only explicit operating preferences may be durable. Do not persist personality, motives, identity, emotional profile, or persona assumptions by default.

steps: read memory_summary, _META_INDEX, or router manifest first
select one category index, point index, or outer_retrieval_surface
open only the matching capsule, ERR point, SOL point, or status payload
memory_retrieval_chain.missing_meta_rule: If no meta summary exists, use the smallest available top-level index or manifest as a temporary meta layer, mark the missing meta layer as an adaptation gap, and avoid broad memory/history scans.

dynamic_evaluation_governance.cost_rule: Use the cheapest sufficient route; do not load all memory, all skills, or all history because the dynamic layer is active.
dynamic_evaluation_governance.failure_rule: If dynamic evaluation cannot be completed, mark the final answer as dynamically incomplete or unverified.

conversation_linking_contract.active_lookup_scope_rule: Latest means latest active memory inside the current project lane or PROJECTLESS lane, never global latest across unrelated lanes.

mandatory_advisory_control_plane.purpose: make routing, dynamic evaluation, and constitution governance continuously effective without wrapping every tool call
mandatory_advisory_control_plane.cost_rule: Use L0 only for ordinary chat; use lightweight receipts for nontrivial work; run script gates only for ambiguous boundaries, R4/R5, strong claims, memory writes, or explicit hardening.
mandatory_advisory_control_plane.failure_rule: If the control plane was skipped, downgrade final claims and say which boundary was not checked.

search_and_learning_decision_matrix.purpose: avoid closed-door invention and assumption-filled external claims

official_authority_source_search.boundary: Use official or authority sources first for drift-prone public facts; cross-check when practical.

github_open_source_repository_search.boundary: Separate README intent, source-code facts, release or issue evidence, activity signals, and license boundaries.

general_web_cross_check.boundary: Use independent sources when practical; mark source limits when evidence is thin.

source_grounded_learning_intake.boundary: Build a source ledger, classify material, preserve non-applicable boundaries, and require local validation before adoption-success claims.

search_and_learning_decision_matrix.anti_closed_door_rule: For external mechanisms, public repositories, current facts, or unfamiliar methods, do not rely on local reasoning alone. Search or mark the result as unverified.

human_confirmation_permit.rule: A permit is only a short-lived single-event representation of explicit human confirmation for one exact R5/hard-tool event. When it passes for a concrete tool event, the runtime records a used-ledger entry so the same permit/task/tool combination cannot be replayed before expiry. It must not create a session-wide or project-wide bypass; all other R5 events remain blocked.

runtime_enforcement.activation_boundary: The runtime is hard-enforcing only for selected critical boundaries when agent hooks, wrappers, or tool-call paths invoke these scripts before work or tool use. Ordinary tool calls should stay on the advisory control plane.

claim_schema_contract.rule: Claims may pass schema completeness only when source_type and evidence_boundary are enumerated. Strong final phrases require a claim whose evidence boundary is at least local regression strength.

gate_enforcement_boundary: These gates are hard runtime checks when called through hook/wrapper/tool proxy scripts. They still cannot enforce sandbox-level stops if the caller bypasses the runtime entry scripts.
